title: 2023 Post-enumeration Survey | Stats NZ
content:
## 2023 Post-enumeration Survey
23 April 2024, 11:45am
The 2023 Post-enumeration Survey (PES) measures the completeness of the 2023 Census count. This allows population data produced after the census to be updated for the people who are missed in the census, counted more than once, or counted in error, as measured by PES.
The PES collected information in face-to-face interviews from people in around 16,500 selected dwellings during June to August 2023.
Results from the 2023 PES were published on the Stats NZ website on 9 December 2024. These include census response and coverage rates (undercount, overcount, and net coverage) for the total population and a range of sub-populations (including age and ethnic groups). A comprehensive coverage report will be published in April 2025, detailing the survey methodology and providing additional data analysis and insights.
The following methods papers and privacy impact assessments were published during 2023.

## 2023 Post-enumeration Survey: Methodology
Presents background information, operational and methodological details, and key results from Aotearoa New Zealand’s 2023 Post-enumeration Survey (PES).

## 2023 Post-enumeration Survey: Final linking methodology
Explains the final 2023 Post-enumeration Survey linking design and changes made to the design described in 2023 Post-enumeration Survey: Linking design. It also provides the final 2023 Post-enumeration Survey linking results.

## 2023 Post-enumeration Survey: Adaptive design for coverage estimation
Discusses the adaptive design developed by the 2023 Census Coverage project for the 2023 Post-enumeration Survey.

## Over-sampling Māori and Pacific ethnicities – supplement to 2023 Post-enumeration Survey: Standard design for coverage estimation
Compares the distribution of Māori descent, Māori ethnicity, Pacific ethnic group, and Asian ethnic group in non-sampled and sampled primary sampling units (PSUs) in 2023 PES.

## Update on differences in reporting of ethnicity – supplement to 2023 Post-enumeration Survey: Standard design for coverage estimation
Describes analysis of ethnicity using a subset of Post-enumeration Survey (PES) including almost 26,670 records where both 2018 PES and 2018 Census have provided a valid response to ethnicity.

## 2023 Post-enumeration Survey: High-level design
Outlines the key methodological elements of the 2023 Post-enumeration Survey and how they will be implemented.

## 2023 Post-enumeration Survey: Standard design for coverage estimation
Outlines the planned standard design for coverage estimation of the 2023 Census using the 2023 Post-enumeration Survey.

## 2023 Post-enumeration Survey: Linking design
Overview of the research and process undertaken by the 2023 Census Coverage project to design methodology for linking Post-enumeration Survey responses to their corresponding census records.

## Privacy impact assessments

## 2023 Post-enumeration Survey: Privacy impact assessment
Identifies and assesses the key privacy risks associated with running the 2023 Post-enumeration Survey.

## 2023 Post-enumeration Survey clerical linking tool: Privacy impact assessment
Reports the key privacy risks associated with using a Stats NZ built tool to manually link 2023 Post-enumeration Survey responses with 2023 Census records.
About the Post-enumeration Survey – information about selection of households in the PES.
Why do we do the Post-enumeration  Survey (PES), and why is it hard?
Post-enumeration survey 2018: Methods and results
